<!-- Design artifact for issue #221 — generated by the design workflow (7 research + synthesis + 3 adversarial critics + revision). docs(internal). -->

# Design — Issue #221: Quality Goals · Quality Ladder · Enforce Resolution Ceiling

## 1. Problem statement + acceptance checklist

Today a Quality Goal's `resolutionCeiling` (`720p | 1080p | 2160p`) only reshapes **custom-format scores** (via `ceilingGate` in `policy.ts` and `decide` in `engine.ts`). It never enables, disables, or sets the cutoff on `quality_profile_qualities`. This issue makes the ceiling additionally produce an explicit, previewable **quality-ladder** configuration (enabled set + one cutoff), derived per `arr_type`, applied through the standard guarded PCD ops model, using **one op builder shared by preview and apply**.

Because `quality_profile_qualities` is a **single shared row set with no `arr_type` column**, a per-arr ceiling reshaping the ladder inherently reshapes it for **every** Arr that syncs the profile. This design treats that as a first-class, surfaced property (§3.4, §5) rather than something the value guard silently prevents.

Acceptance criteria as a checklist:

- [ ] **AC1** — Each supported ceiling maps to an explicit per-Arr enabled-quality set and a single cutoff, derived from `quality_api_mappings` + `QUALITIES[arrType]` (no sibling assumptions).
- [ ] **AC2** — Preview shows every quality-ladder and cutoff change before apply (surfaced through `configDiff` + a new `plan.qualityLadder` render section, including the shared-ladder advisory).
- [ ] **AC3** — Apply persists the previewed ladder through standard value-guarded PCD ops, sharing the exact SQL builder preview used; the persisted ladder is echoed back via `configDiff` on the apply response.
- [ ] **AC4** — **Genuinely ambiguous** mappings fail fast (HTTP 422) **before any write** — no partial writes. A profile that simply has no derivable ladder (no cutoff row, no mapped qualities) is **not** ambiguous: it returns the CF-score plan with `ladderInput = null`.
- [ ] **AC5** — Existing CF-score generation stays deterministic and transparent (the ladder is an additive, independent output of the same pure engine; `computeGoalPlan` stays total for the CF path).

Out of scope (restated in §8): Lidarr goal application, natural-language goal input, background reapplication of stale bindings, ladder reorder.

---

## 2. The single shared op builder

### 2.1 Name & location

`buildQualityLadderOps` — **new file** `packages/praxrr-app/src/lib/server/pcd/entities/qualityProfiles/qualities/buildQualityOps.ts`.

It is a pure extraction of the op-building logic currently inlined in `updateQualities` (`qualities/update.ts:311-426`). After extraction, `updateQualities` becomes a thin persister that calls `buildQualityLadderOps` then a **single batched `writeOperation`**.

> **Batching (finding 10):** The ladder MUST persist as **one** batched `writeOperation` — exactly like today's `updateQualities` — **not** per-op like `updateScoring`. The clear-before-set ordering for `idx_one_upgrade_until_per_profile` and the full-list `desiredState` consumed by `checkFullListConflict` / `qualityProfileQualitiesRowRule` only hold when all row queries land in one op with one recompile. The analogy to the `buildScoringOps`/`updateScoring` split is **extraction only** (pure builder + thin persister); it is **not** an analogy to `updateScoring`'s per-op write loop. `buildQualityLadderOps` returns both the ordered per-row ops (for the sandbox to apply query-by-query) and the single batched payload (for persist), and a characterization test asserts `batched.queries === ops.flatMap(o => o.queries)` in the same clear-before-set order.

### 2.2 Exact TypeScript signature

```ts
import type { PCDCache } from '$pcd/index.ts';
import type { OperationLayer } from '$pcd/index.ts';
import type { OrderedItem } from '$shared/pcd/display.ts';
import type { CompiledQuery } from 'kysely';

export interface UpdateQualitiesInput {
  // now EXPORTED from qualities/update.ts
  orderedItems: OrderedItem[];
}

export interface QualityLadderRowOp {
  // == existing internal QualityRowOp shape
  description: string;
  queries: CompiledQuery[];
  desiredState: Record<string, unknown>; // per-row { ordered_items: { mode, key, from, to } }
  changedFields: string[]; // ['quality_item:<type>:<name>']
  summary: string;
  title: string;
}

export interface BuiltQualityLadder {
  /** Ordered: removes, adds, then updates sorted so upgrade_until clears precede sets. */
  ops: QualityLadderRowOp[];
  /** Single-writeOperation payload; null when the ladder is a no-op. */
  batched: {
    queries: CompiledQuery[];
    desiredState: Record<string, unknown>; // full-list { ordered_items: { from, to } }
    changedFields: string[];
  } | null;
}

export interface BuildQualityLadderOptions {
  databaseId: number;
  cache: PCDCache; // current ladder read comes from THIS cache
  layer: OperationLayer;
  profileName: string;
  input: UpdateQualitiesInput; // desired ladder = plan.ladderInput
  /** Goal path only: reject a ladder that would REMOVE any current row (defensive). */
  forbidRemovals?: boolean;
}

export async function buildQualityLadderOps(
  options: BuildQualityLadderOptions
): Promise<BuiltQualityLadder | { error: string }>;
```

Inputs consumed: the desired `orderedItems` (from `plan.ladderInput`, see §4) plus the **current** ladder, which the builder reads from the **caller-supplied cache** via `qualities(cache, databaseId, profileName)` (`qualities/read.ts:11`). The current-ladder read produces the value guards (old `position`/`enabled`/`upgrade_until`). **Which cache is passed matters for preview↔apply parity — see §4.4.**

Outputs: guarded INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE `CompiledQuery`s for `quality_profile_qualities`. For the goal path only **UPDATE** ops are produced (positions/rows preserved — see §3.4). The extracted builder keeps add/remove capability so `updateQualities`' existing callers are unaffected, but the goal path passes `forbidRemovals: true` so that if the desired `orderedItems` ever fails to cover a current row (a bug), the builder returns `{ error }` instead of emitting a DELETE — a defensive backstop for the invariant in §3.4/finding 4/finding 9. It enforces the single-`upgrade_until` invariant (`update.ts:314-317`) and preserves the cutoff-ordering sort (`update.ts:373-379`) required by the partial unique index `idx_one_upgrade_until_per_profile` (`0.schema.sql:560-563`).

> The per-Arr ceiling→ladder **decision** lives in the pure engine (`buildCeilingLadder`, §3). `buildQualityLadderOps` is deliberately arr-agnostic: it only turns a desired `OrderedItem[]` into guarded SQL. This keeps the SQL builder reusable by both the goal path and the existing manual qualities editor.

### 2.3 How both preview AND apply call it (no SQL divergence)

- **Apply** (`routes/api/v1/goals/apply/+server.ts`): does **not** call `updateQualities` directly. It calls the combined persister `persistGoalApply` (§4.6), which internally calls `buildQualityLadderOps(...)` (for the ladder SQL) and `buildScoringOps(...)` (for scoring SQL) against the **real** cache, merges them into **one** `writeOperation`, and persists via the guard-enforcing path (§5).
- **Preview** (`pcd/sandbox/withSandboxCache.ts`): inside the per-profile `SAVEPOINT`, after building/applying scoring ops, it calls the **same** `buildQualityLadderOps(...)` against the **sandbox** cache and applies each `built.ops[*].queries` to the ephemeral SQLite handle within the same savepoint.

Both paths compile the ladder SQL from the identical `plan.ladderInput` through `buildQualityLadderOps` — the invariant the existing scoring path holds (`buildScoringOps` shared by `updateScoring` and `withSandboxCache`). The **merged multi-key desiredState** used for guard evaluation (§5) is apply-only; a parity test (§7 AC3) asserts the ladder queries preview applies are byte-identical to the ladder portion of apply's combined op.

---

## 3. Per-Arr ceiling → ladder mapping (pure engine)

### 3.1 Where it lives

**New file** `packages/praxrr-app/src/lib/shared/goals/ladder.ts` — pure, versioned (like `policy.ts`). It never imports `$sync/mappings.ts` (server-only) or the DB. The per-arr resolution numbers are **injected as data** (`GoalQualityFact[]`, materialized on the server, §4). Only the arr-agnostic PCD "junk" name set and the ceiling rule are static here.

```ts
import type { OrderedItem } from '$shared/pcd/display.ts';
import type { GoalResolutionCeiling } from './types.ts';

/** One quality that EXISTS for the target arr, with its resolution from QUALITIES[arrType]. */
export interface GoalQualityFact {
  name: string; // EXACT-CASE PCD canonical quality name (quality_api_mappings.quality_name)
  resolution: number; // QUALITIES[arrType][apiName].resolution
}

export interface GoalQualityLadderItem {
  name: string;
  type: 'quality' | 'group';
  enabled: boolean;
  upgradeUntil: boolean;
  position: number;
  resolution: number | null; // null = unmapped for arr (orphan) or all-orphan/all-junk group
  mapped: boolean; // false = not present/derivable for target arr → left UNCHANGED
}

export interface GoalQualityLadder {
  ceiling: GoalResolutionCeiling;
  cutoff: string | null; // PCD name carrying upgrade_until; null when no ladder change
  items: GoalQualityLadderItem[];
  /** True when this profile is also compatible with a sibling arr → the reshape is shared. */
  reshapesSiblingArrs: boolean;
  /** Human advisory surfaced in preview + decision log when a ladder change is produced. */
  sharedLadderNote: string | null;
}

export class GoalLadderMappingError extends Error {
  /** Only genuinely-ambiguous cases are fatal. */
  readonly code: 'straddling_group';
}

/**
 * PURE. Returns ladderInput=null when NO ladder change is derivable (additive, non-fatal).
 * Throws GoalLadderMappingError ONLY for genuine ambiguity (a group whose mapped, non-junk
 * members straddle the ceiling).
 */
export function buildCeilingLadder(
  ceiling: GoalResolutionCeiling,
  currentLadder: OrderedItem[],
  facts: GoalQualityFact[],
  compatibleWithSiblingArr: boolean
): {
  ladderInput: { orderedItems: OrderedItem[] } | null;
  ladder: GoalQualityLadder;
};
```

### 3.2 Static junk set (PCD canonical names, arr-agnostic)

`JUNK_QUALITIES` (never auto-enabled regardless of resolution):
`Unknown, WORKPRINT, CAM, TELESYNC, TELECINE, DVDSCR, REGIONAL, BR-DISK, Raw-HD`.

These are **PCD canonical names**, not per-arr API names, so listing them in `$shared` does not duplicate the per-arr vocabulary in `mappings.ts` (DRY guardrail honored). Per-arr existence is still enforced by the injected `facts` (a junk name only appears if it maps for the arr). `BR-DISK`/`Raw-HD` (res 1080) and `CAM`/etc. (res 0) are excluded here rather than tiered by their raw resolution number.

### 3.3 Enable/cutoff rule (single source both preview & apply consult)

Ceiling → numeric threshold: `720p→720`, `1080p→1080`, `2160p→2160`.

For each **quality** fact `q` (mapped for the arr):

- `enabled = q.name ∉ JUNK_QUALITIES && q.resolution > 0 && q.resolution <= ceilingResolution`.

Cutoff = the `Bluray-<ceiling>` row (`Bluray-720p` / `Bluray-1080p` / `Bluray-2160p`) — same PCD name in both arrs. If a `Bluray-<ceiling>` quality row **exists and is mapped** in the current ladder, exactly one `upgrade_until=1` is set on it (partial unique index). If it does **not** exist, **no cutoff is set and no ladder change is produced** — `ladderInput = null` (see §3.5; this is _not_ an error).

Derived per-Arr result (from `QUALITIES` resolutions verified in code):

**RADARR** (`QUALITIES.radarr`, mappings.ts:234-265)

| Ceiling | ENABLED (`enabled=1`)                                                                                                | CUTOFF (`upgrade_until=1`) | DISABLED                     |
| ------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | ---------------------------- |
| 720p    | SDTV, DVD, DVD-R, WEBDL-480p, WEBRip-480p, Bluray-480p, Bluray-576p, HDTV-720p, WEBDL-720p, WEBRip-720p, Bluray-720p | **Bluray-720p**            | all 1080p + all 2160p + JUNK |
| 1080p   | 720p set + HDTV-1080p, WEBDL-1080p, WEBRip-1080p, Bluray-1080p, Remux-1080p                                          | **Bluray-1080p**           | all 2160p + JUNK             |
| 2160p   | 1080p set + HDTV-2160p, WEBDL-2160p, WEBRip-2160p, Bluray-2160p, Remux-2160p                                         | **Bluray-2160p**           | JUNK only                    |

JUNK(radarr) = Unknown, WORKPRINT, CAM, TELESYNC, TELECINE, DVDSCR, REGIONAL, BR-DISK, Raw-HD.

**SONARR** (`QUALITIES.sonarr`, mappings.ts:266-299 — no WORKPRINT/CAM/TELESYNC/TELECINE/DVDSCR/REGIONAL/DVD-R/BR-DISK)

| Ceiling | ENABLED (`enabled=1`)                                                                                         | CUTOFF (`upgrade_until=1`) | DISABLED                     |
| ------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | ---------------------------- |
| 720p    | SDTV, DVD, WEBDL-480p, WEBRip-480p, Bluray-480p, Bluray-576p, HDTV-720p, WEBDL-720p, WEBRip-720p, Bluray-720p | **Bluray-720p**            | all 1080p + all 2160p + JUNK |
| 1080p   | 720p set + HDTV-1080p, WEBDL-1080p, WEBRip-1080p, Bluray-1080p, Remux-1080p                                   | **Bluray-1080p**           | all 2160p + JUNK             |
| 2160p   | 1080p set + HDTV-2160p, WEBDL-2160p, WEBRip-2160p, Bluray-2160p, Remux-2160p                                  | **Bluray-2160p**           | JUNK only                    |

JUNK(sonarr) = Unknown, Raw-HD.

Remux naming: the builder emits **PCD names** `Remux-1080p`/`Remux-2160p` in both arrs. `quality_api_mappings` translates to `Bluray-1080p Remux`/`Bluray-2160p Remux` for Sonarr at sync time (2.qualities.sql:98-102). **The op builder MUST NEVER write Sonarr API names into `quality_profile_qualities`.** Consequently `GoalQualityFact.name` for the Sonarr remux row is `Remux-1080p` (the PCD `quality_name`), _not_ `Bluray-1080p Remux`, and its resolution is `QUALITIES.sonarr['Bluray-1080p Remux'].resolution === 1080` (§4.3, finding 5).

### 3.4 Shared-ladder / cross-Arr rule (critical — full-coverage invariant)

`quality_profile_qualities` has **no `arr_type` column** — the ladder is one shared row set filtered at sync. Two hard requirements follow.

**(A) Full-coverage invariant (findings 4 + 9).** When `ladderInput` is non-null, `buildCeilingLadder` MUST return `ladderInput.orderedItems` containing **every** row of the current ladder, in current order:

- rows whose PCD name has a `GoalQualityFact` (mapped, non-junk quality, or a group with mapped non-junk members) are emitted with their **new** `enabled`/`upgradeUntil`, keeping their current `position` and identity;
- **every other current row** — an unmapped-for-arr quality (e.g. Radarr-only `DVD-R` under a Sonarr goal, or the orphan `HDTV-480p` mapped for neither arr), a junk quality, an all-orphan/all-junk group — is **cloned verbatim** (same `position`, `enabled`, `upgradeUntil`, `members`).

Because `buildQualityLadderOps` derives removals by set difference (`removedItems = currentData.orderedItems.filter(item => !nextByKey.has(rowKey(item)))`, `update.ts:323`) and the full-list `desiredState.to` from `input.orderedItems` (`update.ts:406`), **omitting any current row would emit a guarded DELETE and drop the row from the full-list `to`** — silent, catastrophic cross-arr data loss on the shared row set. Full coverage guarantees **zero** removals and a full-list `to` that still contains every sibling-arr-only row byte-identical. The goal path additionally sets `forbidRemovals: true` (§2.2) as a defensive backstop, and §7 adds a characterization test asserting a Sonarr goal against a Radarr-populated shared profile emits **zero** DELETE/remove ops and leaves all Radarr-only rows unchanged.

This makes standalone-orphan and group-member handling **consistent** (finding 2): a sibling-only quality is treated identically whether it is a standalone row or a group member — excluded from the ceiling computation and preserved verbatim, never a fatal error.

**(B) The reshape is inherently shared; the value guard does NOT prevent cross-arr collisions (finding 3).** Rows mapped for **both** arrs (e.g. `Bluray-1080p`, `HDTV-720p`) are genuinely reshaped by a per-arr ceiling goal, and that reshape applies to every Arr syncing the profile. The value guard is a **TOCTOU concurrency guard** (it fires only when the _live_ row differs from the op's `from` snapshot within the plan-read→write window), **not** a cross-arr semantic detector: a normal sequence "apply Radarr 720p goal, then apply Sonarr 1080p goal" reads post-Radarr state, matches its guards, and legitimately overwrites the shared rows (last-write-wins). We therefore **do not** claim the guard prevents this. Instead:

- `buildCeilingLadder` computes `reshapesSiblingArrs` from `compatibleWithSiblingArr` (materialized on the server via `computeCompatibleProfileNames`, §4.3): true when the target profile is also compatible with a sibling `arr_type`.
- When `ladderInput !== null` and `reshapesSiblingArrs`, `sharedLadderNote` is set to an explicit advisory ("This profile's quality ladder is shared by every Arr that syncs it; applying this ceiling changes the enabled qualities and cutoff for those Arr instances too, not just <arrType>."). Preview surfaces it prominently (§4.5) and the decision log records it (§6). This is honest transparency in place of a false safety claim.

Positions are never reordered by the goal path (reorder is intentionally out of scope; §8).

### 3.5 Failure vs. no-op classification (finding 12 — engine stays total for CF)

`computeGoalPlan` MUST remain **total** for the CF-score path. A profile with a partial or empty ladder is a **valid** profile, not an ambiguous mapping. Therefore:

**Non-fatal → `ladderInput = null`, CF plan still returned** (`qualityLadder.cutoff = null`, `qualityLadder.items` describes intent for display, no ladder ops emitted):

- **No `Bluray-<ceiling>` quality row** present/mapped in the current ladder (former `missing_cutoff`).
- **No mapped, non-junk quality at/below the ceiling** exists in the ladder (former `empty_enabled_set`).
- A group whose members are **all junk** or **all unmapped-for-arr** (former `unmapped_group_member`) — preserved verbatim per §3.4(A), never fatal.
- Any standalone unmapped-for-arr or junk quality — preserved verbatim.

**Fatal → HTTP 422, before any write (genuine ambiguity only):**

- **Ambiguous mapping** — a `quality_api_mappings` row for the arr whose `api_name` has **no** `QUALITIES[arrType]` resolution entry. Detected in the server materializer (§4.3), which throws `error(422)` directly.
- **Straddling group** — a group whose **mapped, non-junk** members straddle the ceiling (some `<=`, some `>`). This is the only `GoalLadderMappingError` (`code: 'straddling_group'`), thrown from the pure engine and translated to 422 in `buildGoalPlan`.

Group enable rule (finding 1 + finding 2 + open-decision 4): for a group with at least one mapped, non-junk member, compute the decision from **only** its mapped, non-junk members: `enabled = maxMappedNonJunkResolution <= ceiling`; if those members straddle → `straddling_group` (422). Junk/unmapped members inside such a group are ignored for the decision but preserved as members of the row. A group with **no** mapped, non-junk member is passed through verbatim (non-fatal).

**Verified against the real default profile (finding 1):** `create.ts` builds three groups — `WEB 1080p` (`WEBDL-1080p`, `WEBRip-1080p`), `Pre-releases` (`REGIONAL, DVDSCR, TELECINE, TELESYNC, CAM, WORKPRINT`), and `Unwanted` (`Unknown, Raw-HD, BR-DISK`) — with `Remux-1080p`/`Bluray-1080p` as **individual** enabled qualities, **not** a group (the previous draft's "Bluray-1080p/Remux-1080p grouping" was factually wrong). Under this rule: `Pre-releases` (all junk in Radarr; all unmapped in Sonarr) and `Unwanted` (all junk; `BR-DISK` also Sonarr-unmapped) are **passed through verbatim** for both arrs — **no 422**. `WEB 1080p`'s members are mapped, non-junk 1080p qualities in both arrs; they don't straddle, so the group is enabled at a 1080p+ ceiling and disabled at 720p. §7 adds golden apply tests for both Radarr and Sonarr goals against the exact `create.ts` default profile proving no 422 and correct enable/cutoff.

The materializer additionally throws 422 on the ambiguous-mapping case. **No sibling inference anywhere.**

---

## 4. End-to-end flow of ladder + cutoff changes

### 4.1 Engine plan type extension (`$shared/goals/types.ts`)

```ts
// ComputeGoalPlanInput gains:
qualityFacts: GoalQualityFact[];          // per-arr, materialized on server
currentLadder: OrderedItem[];             // profile's current quality_profile_qualities
compatibleWithSiblingArr: boolean;        // materialized: profile also usable by another arr

// GoalPlan gains:
/** Internal, stripped before wire (analogue of scoringInput). null when no ladder change. */
ladderInput: { orderedItems: OrderedItem[] } | null;
/** Wire-exposed, presentational (includes reshapesSiblingArrs + sharedLadderNote). */
qualityLadder: GoalQualityLadder;
```

`GOALS_ENGINE_VERSION` bumps `'1' → '2'` (types.ts:22) — ceiling semantics now produce ladder output; apply enforces `expectedEngineVersion` (409 on mismatch), and golden tests pin it. Rewrite the `resolutionCeiling` axis description (`types.ts:150-151`) — it currently says the ceiling "does not disable quality-ladder items", which becomes false.

### 4.2 `computeGoalPlan` (`$shared/goals/engine.ts`)

CF-score logic (`decide`, `ceilingGate`) is **unchanged** (AC5). After building `decisions`/`thresholds`/`scoringInput`, it calls `buildCeilingLadder(input.weights.resolutionCeiling, input.currentLadder, input.qualityFacts, input.compatibleWithSiblingArr)` and attaches `ladderInput` + `qualityLadder`. `buildCeilingLadder` returns `ladderInput: null` for every non-fatal "no ladder derivable" case (§3.5), so the CF path is **never** blocked by a partial/empty ladder. It throws `GoalLadderMappingError('straddling_group')` **only** for a genuinely ambiguous group; that propagates out of `computeGoalPlan` (deterministic, pure — same convention as `buildScoringOps` throwing on a bad increment).

### 4.3 Server materialization (`packages/praxrr-app/src/lib/server/goals/`)

**New** `materializeQualityFacts.ts` — the read boundary mirroring `materializeCfFacts.ts`:

```ts
export async function materializeQualityFacts(
  cache: PCDCache,
  arrType: GoalArrType
): Promise<GoalQualityFact[]>;
```

Reads `quality_api_mappings` where `arr_type = arrType`. For each row it MUST (finding 5):

1. set `fact.name` to the **exact-case** `quality_name` column (the PCD canonical name that matches `OrderedItem.name` from `read.ts`, e.g. `Bluray-1080p`, `Remux-1080p`) — **never** lowercased, **never** the `api_name`;
2. resolve `fact.resolution` via `QUALITIES[arrType][api_name].resolution` (e.g. Sonarr remux: `QUALITIES.sonarr['Bluray-1080p Remux'].resolution === 1080`);
3. **throw `error(422)`** — not silently `continue` — when an `api_name` has **no** `QUALITIES[arrType]` entry (ambiguous mapping).

It therefore **does not** reuse `computeCompatibleProfileNames` verbatim (that helper lowercases names and skips unresolved rows — both wrong here). It may share only the join shape, not the case-folding/skip behavior. Absence of a `quality_api_mappings` row for a name = that quality does not exist for the arr → it simply does not appear in `facts` (and its current-ladder row is preserved verbatim per §3.4(A)).

`compatibleWithSiblingArr` is computed once on the server: true when the target profile's enabled/mapped quality names are also compatible with any sibling `arr_type` (reusing the existing `computeCompatibleProfileNames` path, which is appropriate here because it is a case-insensitive _compatibility_ check, not a fact source).

`buildGoalPlan` (`planRequest.ts:91-116`) extends to fetch the current ladder + facts + sibling-compat flag and pass them into `computeGoalPlan`:

```ts
const currentLadder = (
  await qualities(cache, request.databaseId, request.profileName)
).orderedItems;
const qualityFacts = await materializeQualityFacts(cache, request.arrType);
const compatibleWithSiblingArr = await isProfileCompatibleWithSiblingArr(
  cache,
  request
);
const plan = computeGoalPlan({
  arrType,
  weights,
  presetBaseUpgrade,
  customFormats,
  qualityFacts,
  currentLadder,
  compatibleWithSiblingArr,
});
```

Wrap `computeGoalPlan` so a `GoalLadderMappingError` (only `straddling_group`) becomes `error(422, ...)`. Because **both** preview and apply call `buildGoalPlan`, genuinely ambiguous mappings fail identically and **before any write** (AC4); a merely partial ladder yields `ladderInput = null` and a normal 200 CF plan (AC5).

`toWirePlan` (`responses.ts:14-17`) strips the new internal field:

```ts
const { scoringInput: _s, ladderInput: _l, ...wire } = plan; // qualityLadder stays on the wire
```

### 4.4 Preview response + sandbox (baseline parity, finding 8)

`ProfileEdit` (`withSandboxCache.ts:45-48`) gains an optional ladder input:

```ts
export interface ProfileEdit {
  input: UpdateScoringInput;
  ladderInput?: UpdateQualitiesInput;
  changes: GoalLadderChange[] | ProposedChange[];
}
```

Two parity requirements:

1. **Same baseline (finding 8, secondary).** The sandbox base MUST be the **resolved** cache — the same guard-evaluated state `build()` produces and that apply writes against — **not** a raw `buildReadOnly` snapshot that retains conflicted/dropped user ops. Concretely: the preview harness clones the sandbox from the resolved cache used by `pcdManager.getCache()`, so the current ladder the sandbox presents is byte-identical to the ladder apply reads. `buildCeilingLadder`'s `currentLadder` (from `buildGoalPlan`) and the sandbox's current-ladder read then agree, so `buildQualityLadderOps` computes identical ops in preview and apply.

2. **Guard-clean projection surfaced (finding 8, primary).** In the sandbox loop (`withSandboxCache.ts:82-135`), inside the **same** `SAVEPOINT` used for scoring, after applying scoring ops call `buildQualityLadderOps({ databaseId, cache: sandboxCache, layer:'user', profileName, input: edit.ladderInput, forbidRemovals:true })` and apply each `built.ops[*].queries`. For every guarded ladder UPDATE, **check the affected row count**; a **0-row** result (an upstream/sibling change moved the guarded old values) is recorded as a **`skippedChange` (conflict)** rather than silently shown as "no change" — matching apply's conflict outcome (§5). A `{ error }` result skips the profile.

`toProfileEdit.ts` synthesizes ladder change entries and sets `ladderInput: plan.ladderInput`. **`appliedChanges`/`skippedChanges` for the ladder are derived from the emitted ops, not from the desired ladder (finding 13):** `toProfileEdit` builds the ladder ops (via `buildQualityLadderOps` against the same resolved cache) and emits exactly one change entry per **actually-changed** row, so `appliedChanges` is 1:1 with `configDiff`. A cutoff move is represented as **two** `set_quality_cutoff` entries carrying `from`/`to` (old cutoff row `true→false`, new cutoff row `false→true`), matching the two `upgradeUntil` `FieldChange`s `configDiff` surfaces; enabled flips are `set_quality_enabled` entries carrying `from`/`to`. Rows whose ops were not emitted produce no entry.

Because `serializeQualityProfile` includes `orderedItems` (`serialize.ts:165`) and `PORTABLE_ARRAY_KEY_STRATEGIES` keys `orderedItems` by `name` (`layerDiff.ts:72-78`), `buildQualityProfileConfigDiff` (`configDiff.ts:26`) **automatically** surfaces every enable/disable/cutoff mutation as `FieldChange`s under `orderedItems["<name>"].enabled` / `orderedItems["<name>"].upgradeUntil` (camelCase) once the sandbox applies ladder ops — no diff-schema change required (AC2).

**OpenAPI additions — do NOT widen the shared union (finding 11).** The impact-simulator's `ProposedChange` `oneOf` is the **input** contract for `/api/v1/simulate/impact`, whose runtime validator `validateProposedChange` only accepts `set_cf_score`/`set_profile_setting`. Widening it would document kinds the validator rejects (400) and the impact `ProfileEdit` builder cannot apply. Instead:

- **`docs/api/v1/schemas/goals.yaml`:** add a **goals-only** `GoalLadderChange` `oneOf` with two variants — `SetQualityEnabledChange` (`kind:'set_quality_enabled'`, `{profileName, qualityName, qualityType, from, to}`) and `SetQualityCutoffChange` (`kind:'set_quality_cutoff'`, `{profileName, qualityName, qualityType, from, to}`) — referenced **only** by `GoalPreviewResponse.appliedChanges`/`skippedChanges` (and, per below, `GoalApplyResponse`).
- **`docs/api/v1/schemas/impact-simulator.yaml`:** unchanged.
- `GoalPlan` (`goals.yaml`) gains `qualityLadder` (object: `ceiling`, `cutoff` nullable, `reshapesSiblingArrs` boolean, `sharedLadderNote` nullable, `items[]` of `{name,type,enabled,upgradeUntil,position,resolution,mapped}`).
- `GoalApplyResponse` (`goals.yaml:366-375`) gains `configDiff` (finding 17) so apply echoes the persisted ladder+scoring diff (transparency parity with preview).
- Internal TS: the shared sandbox `describeChange` helper (`impact-simulator/[databaseId]/helpers.ts:133-140`) is made **exhaustive** to cover the two new kinds (otherwise it renders "set undefined to undefined"). The impact route's `validateProposedChange` continues to reject the ladder kinds by allowlist — a contract test asserts a ladder kind posted to `/api/v1/simulate/impact` is rejected/skipped, proving schema and runtime stay in lockstep.

Then regen: `deno task generate:api-types` (→ `v1.d.ts`) and `deno task bundle:api` (→ `praxrr-api/openapi.json` + `types.ts`), then `prettier --write packages/praxrr-api/openapi.json` (it is prettier-gated), then `deno task check`. Commit only the intended `v1.d.ts` deltas (regen emits tool-version noise that CI does not gate).

### 4.5 `GeneratedConfig.svelte` preview section

`packages/praxrr-app/src/lib/client/ui/goals/GeneratedConfig.svelte` gains a full-width "Quality ladder & cutoff" `Card` inserted between the summary grid (closes ~line 77) and the CF-scores table (opens ~line 80), reading `plan.qualityLadder.items` — a table of qualities showing enabled/disabled with the `cutoff` row highlighted. When `plan.qualityLadder.sharedLadderNote` is non-null it renders a prominent advisory banner in that card (the shared-ladder reshape, §3.4(B)). When `plan.qualityLadder.cutoff === null` the card shows "No ladder change (profile has no derivable cutoff for this ceiling)". The existing Thresholds card also surfaces `plan.qualityLadder.cutoff`. `changeCount` (`+page.svelte:49`, derived from `configDiff[0].changes.length`) auto-inflates because ladder mutations are ordinary `FieldChange`s — no client wiring change needed for the count.

### 4.6 Apply (single atomic guarded op, findings 6 + 7 + 15)

Goal apply persists scoring **and** ladder as **one** `writeOperation` (finding 7), via the **guard-enforcing** path (finding 6). New server helpers under `server/goals/`:

- `buildGoalApplyOps(cache, layer, profileName, plan)` — pure-ish assembler that calls `buildScoringOps(...)` and (when `plan.ladderInput !== null`) `buildQualityLadderOps(..., forbidRemovals:true)` against the **real** cache, then merges them into one payload: `queries = [...scoringQueries, ...ladderBatched.queries]` (ladder clear-before-set order preserved), and `desiredState = { custom_format_scores: <scoring full-list>, ordered_items: <ladder full-list from/to> }`, `changedFields` = union. When `plan.ladderInput === null`, only the scoring keys are present.
- `persistGoalApply(...)` — thin persister that writes that single op through a path setting **`runValueGuardGate: true`** (mirroring `writeOperationsFromSql`, writer.ts:741-747). Verified: with `runValueGuardGate:true` the writer runs `runValueGuardGate(...)` **before persist** and returns `{ success:false, error }` on `!gateResult.ok` (writer.ts:586-594) — so a guard-mismatched op is **rejected pre-persist**, nothing lands, and the default `override` strategy never gets to re-apply/overwrite it. This is the fix for finding 6: guards are otherwise recompile-time and per-strategy (`override` overwrites, `ask` silently withholds); we opt the goal path out of that by gating at write.

> **Multi-key guard requirement (finding 7).** The value-guard gate MUST evaluate **both** the `ordered_items` full-list rule (`checkFullListConflict` / `qualityProfileQualitiesRowRule`) **and** the `custom_format_scores` scoring row rules on the single merged op. `checkFullListConflict` keys off `desiredState.ordered_items` only, so scoring guards need their own rule to run on the same op. The implementation MUST make the gate iterate **all** `desiredState` key families for one op (not assume one rule per op). §7 adds a characterization test asserting both guard shapes are present on the merged op and that a mismatch on **either** family yields `success:false`.

`GoalApplyDependencies` (`apply/+server.ts:13-25`) and `DEFAULT_DEPENDENCIES` (`:20-25`) gain **`persistGoalApply: typeof persistGoalApply`** (replacing the goal path's direct use of `updateScoring`; `updateScoring`/`updateQualities` remain for their other callers). The test factory `buildApplyDependencies` (`goalsRoutes.test.ts:133-147`) MUST be updated to construct the new field, or all three existing apply tests fail to type-check. The exported handler is **`_handleGoalApplyRequest`** (`apply/+server.ts:32`) — the design text uses that exact symbol. Flow of `_handleGoalApplyRequest`:

1. `buildGoalPlan(goalRequest)` — materializes facts + ladder; **fails fast 422** on genuine ambiguity before any write; returns `ladderInput: null` for merely-partial ladders (no error).
2. `persistGoalApply({ databaseId, cache, layer:'user', profileName, plan })` — **one** guarded `writeOperation`. On `success:false` (guard conflict), return **HTTP 409** (`{ code: 'ladder_conflict' | 'scoring_conflict' }`); nothing has persisted, so no partial write (findings 6+7).
3. On success, recompute and attach `configDiff` (finding 17) from the same `buildQualityProfileConfigDiff` path, then `upsertBinding` + decision log (existing). A test asserts apply's `configDiff` equals preview's for the same request.

See §5 for the guard/atomicity model.

---

## 5. Value-guard strategy & atomicity (corrected model)

**Value guards are recompile-time and per-strategy by default (finding 6).** For an ordinary `writeOperation` (`runValueGuardGate:false`), only `cache.validateSql` runs at write time (writer.ts:562); a guarded UPDATE that matches 0 rows is **not** a SQLite error, so it persists and is reconciled only later at `compile()` recompile, where the default `override` strategy **re-applies** the goal's desired ladder over the upstream/sibling change (override.ts) — the opposite of fail-fast — and `ask` silently withholds (`conflicted_pending`), both still HTTP 200. **We therefore do not use the plain `writeOperation` path for goal apply.**

**Goal apply gates at write (findings 6 + 7).** `persistGoalApply` writes the single merged op through the `runValueGuardGate:true` path. The writer runs the value-guard gate **before persist** (writer.ts:586-594) over the op's guards:

- ladder: `checkFullListConflict` re-reads the live ladder and compares to `desiredState.ordered_items.to`;
- scoring: the scoring row rules compare live scores to `desiredState.custom_format_scores`.

Any mismatch → `gateResult.ok === false` → writer returns `{ success:false }` → **nothing is persisted** → apply returns **409**. This is a genuine TOCTOU concurrency guard: it protects against a manual edit or a sibling arr's goal landing in the plan-read→write window. It is **not** a cross-arr semantic guard — a _sequential_ Radarr-then-Sonarr apply on shared rows is a legitimate last-write-wins reshape, surfaced (not blocked) via `sharedLadderNote` (§3.4(B)). AC3's "persists the previewed ladder" holds only when no upstream change occurred between preview and apply; otherwise apply returns 409 and the client re-previews.

**Cutoff ordering.** The batched ladder payload preserves the sort so `upgrade_until→false` clears run before `upgrade_until→true` sets (`update.ts:373-379`), avoiding a `UNIQUE constraint failed` on `idx_one_upgrade_until_per_profile`.

**Atomicity — no partial writes (finding 7).**

- Scoring **and** ladder persist as **one** `writeOperation` (one `pcd_ops` row, one recompile). `cache.validateSql` dry-runs every statement in a `SAVEPOINT` and rolls back on any failure before persisting (cache.ts:502); the pre-persist guard gate rejects conflicts before any row is written. So the whole goal apply is all-or-nothing — a ladder conflict can no longer leave scoring committed.
- Genuinely ambiguous mappings (AC4) are detected in `buildGoalPlan` **before** persist — no write occurs.
- Preview is atomic per profile via the shared `SAVEPOINT`/`ROLLBACK TO`/`RELEASE`, and its sandbox rowcount check (§4.4) reports the same conflicts apply would hit.

---

## 6. File-by-file change list

**Create**

- `packages/praxrr-app/src/lib/shared/goals/ladder.ts` — pure `buildCeilingLadder`, `GoalQualityFact`, `GoalQualityLadder`, `GoalQualityLadderItem`, `JUNK_QUALITIES`, `GoalLadderMappingError('straddling_group')`. (§3)
- `packages/praxrr-app/src/lib/server/goals/materializeQualityFacts.ts` — server read boundary; exact-case `fact.name`, resolution via `QUALITIES[arrType][api_name]`, fail-fast 422 on ambiguous mapping. (§4.3)
- `packages/praxrr-app/src/lib/server/goals/buildGoalApplyOps.ts` + `persistGoalApply.ts` — merge scoring+ladder into one guarded op; persist via `runValueGuardGate:true`. (§4.6, §5)
- `packages/praxrr-app/src/lib/server/pcd/entities/qualityProfiles/qualities/buildQualityOps.ts` — pure `buildQualityLadderOps` extracted from `updateQualities`; `forbidRemovals` option; returns `ops[]` + single `batched`. (§2)
- `packages/praxrr-app/src/tests/shared/goals/ladder.test.ts` — golden per-arr × per-ceiling tables; default-profile (create.ts) golden for radarr+sonarr (no 422); non-fatal null cases; straddling-group 422; sonarr remux fact test. (§7)
- `packages/praxrr-app/src/tests/pcd/qualities/buildQualityOpsCharacterization.test.ts` — guarded-SQL characterization; zero-removal cross-arr test; `batched.queries === ops.flatMap` assertion. (§7)
- `packages/praxrr-app/src/tests/routes/goalsApplyPersistence.test.ts` — integration apply against a real in-memory `PCDCache`; re-query proves persistence; preview↔apply ladder-op parity. (§7)

**Modify**

- `$shared/goals/types.ts` — extend `ComputeGoalPlanInput` (`qualityFacts`, `currentLadder`, `compatibleWithSiblingArr`) and `GoalPlan` (`ladderInput`, `qualityLadder`); bump `GOALS_ENGINE_VERSION`; rewrite `resolutionCeiling` axis description.
- `$shared/goals/engine.ts` — `computeGoalPlan` calls `buildCeilingLadder`; stays total for CF (null ladder on partial); only `straddling_group` propagates.
- `$shared/goals/index.ts` — re-export ladder types/fn.
- `server/pcd/entities/qualityProfiles/qualities/update.ts` — extract ops logic into `buildQualityOps.ts`; `updateQualities` becomes a thin persister using the single batched `writeOperation`; **export** `UpdateQualitiesInput`.
- `server/pcd/entities/qualityProfiles/qualities/index.ts` — export `buildQualityLadderOps`, `UpdateQualitiesInput`.
- `server/goals/planRequest.ts` — `buildGoalPlan` materializes facts + current ladder + sibling-compat; wrap `computeGoalPlan` → 422 on `GoalLadderMappingError`.
- `server/goals/responses.ts` — `toWirePlan` strips `ladderInput`.
- `server/goals/toProfileEdit.ts` — set `ladderInput`; synthesize **op-derived** ladder changes (1:1 with configDiff; cutoff = paired from/to).
- `server/pcd/sandbox/withSandboxCache.ts` — `ProfileEdit.ladderInput?`; clone sandbox from the **resolved** cache; apply `buildQualityLadderOps` in the shared savepoint; **rowcount check → skippedChange** on 0-row.
- `routes/api/v1/simulate/impact/[databaseId]/helpers.ts` — make `describeChange` exhaustive over the two new kinds (impact route validator/builder unchanged; ladder kinds remain rejected there).
- `routes/api/v1/goals/apply/+server.ts` — add `persistGoalApply` to `GoalApplyDependencies` + `DEFAULT_DEPENDENCIES`; `_handleGoalApplyRequest` persists one guarded op, maps conflict→409, attaches `configDiff`.
- `server/goals/decisionLog.ts` — allowlist a compact ladder summary (`ceiling`, `cutoff`, enabled count, `reshapesSiblingArrs`/shared-ladder note).
- `client/ui/goals/GeneratedConfig.svelte` — add "Quality ladder & cutoff" card + shared-ladder advisory + null-cutoff state.
- `docs/api/v1/schemas/goals.yaml` — `GoalPlan.qualityLadder`; new goals-only `GoalLadderChange` union on `GoalPreviewResponse.appliedChanges/skippedChanges`; `GoalApplyResponse.configDiff` + `appliedChanges`.
- `docs/api/v1/schemas/impact-simulator.yaml` — **unchanged** (shared `ProposedChange` union not widened).
- `packages/praxrr-app/src/lib/api/v1.d.ts` — regenerated (`generate:api-types`).
- `packages/praxrr-api/openapi.json` + `packages/praxrr-api/types.ts` — regenerated (`bundle:api`) then `prettier --write`.
- `packages/praxrr-app/src/tests/shared/goals/engine.test.ts` — pass new `ComputeGoalPlanInput` fields (default: empty facts / current ladder → null ladder, so existing `planFor()` calls stay green); add ladder assertions; bump engineVersion.
- `packages/praxrr-app/src/tests/shared/goals/decisionLog.test.ts` — update `plan()` factory + full-object assertion for the new `GoalPlan` shape.
- `packages/praxrr-app/src/tests/routes/goalsRoutes.test.ts` — extend `SEED_SQL` with `qualities` + `quality_api_mappings` + `quality_profile_qualities`; update `buildApplyDependencies` to include `persistGoalApply`; update `SERVER_GOAL_PLAN`; add preview ladder-diff, apply-persistence, and 422/409 tests.

**Regression note (finding 12):** the existing passing `goalsRoutes.test.ts` "Movies" profile (zero `quality_profile_qualities`) and every `engine.test.ts planFor()` call now return `ladderInput: null` + a normal 200/CF plan — **not** 422 — because a missing cutoff/empty ladder is non-fatal. Fixtures are reseeded only to _add_ ladder coverage, not to mask a regression.

---

## 7. Test plan (AC → concrete test)

Run: `deno task test packages/praxrr-app/src/tests/shared/goals/`, `deno task test packages/praxrr-app/src/tests/routes/`, `deno task test packages/praxrr-app/src/tests/pcd/qualities/`, `deno task check`.

- **AC1** → `tests/shared/goals/ladder.test.ts`:
  - golden `Record`-style assertions (mirror `engine.test.ts:40-57`) for `buildCeilingLadder` over `{radarr,sonarr} × {720p,1080p,2160p}`: exact enabled set + `cutoff = Bluray-<ceiling>`.
  - **Default-profile golden (finding 1):** build the exact `create.ts` default profile (groups `WEB 1080p`, `Pre-releases`, `Unwanted`; individual `Remux-1080p`/`Bluray-1080p`) and assert a Radarr goal **and** a Sonarr goal both succeed (no 422): `Pre-releases`/`Unwanted` preserved verbatim, `WEB 1080p` enabled at 1080p / disabled at 720p, cutoff `Bluray-<ceiling>`.
  - **Sonarr remux fact (finding 5):** assert `materializeQualityFacts(sonarr)` yields a fact with `name === 'Remux-1080p'` (not `'Bluray-1080p Remux'`, not lowercased) and `resolution === 1080`.
  - **Non-fatal null cases (finding 12):** profile lacking `Bluray-<ceiling>` → `ladderInput === null`, `qualityLadder.cutoff === null`; profile with no mapped non-junk quality → `ladderInput === null`.
  - **Straddling group → 422:** a group whose mapped non-junk members straddle the ceiling throws `GoalLadderMappingError('straddling_group')`.
- **AC2** → `goalsRoutes.test.ts` preview test (extend `withGoalsFixture`, `:206-237`): seed a full ladder; assert `body.configDiff[0].changes` contains **camelCase** `orderedItems["<name>"].enabled` / `orderedItems["<name>"].upgradeUntil` `FieldChange`s (finding 16); assert `body.appliedChanges` equals **exactly** the set of rows `configDiff` reports (op-derived, cutoff as paired from/to — finding 13); prove non-persistence by re-querying `quality_profile_qualities` unchanged; assert `qualityLadder.sharedLadderNote` present when the profile is sibling-compatible.
- **AC3** →
  - `tests/pcd/qualities/buildQualityOpsCharacterization.test.ts` (structure from `buildScoringOpsCharacterization.test.ts`): value guards present (`AND enabled = <old>`, `AND position = <old>`, `AND upgrade_until = <old>`); single-`upgrade_until` invariant + clear-before-set ordering; `batched.queries === ops.flatMap(o=>o.queries)`; no-op returns `batched: null`; **zero-removal cross-arr test** — a Sonarr goal against a Radarr-populated shared profile emits **no** DELETE/remove ops and leaves every Radarr-only row (`DVD-R, DVDSCR, REGIONAL, WORKPRINT, CAM, TELESYNC, TELECINE, BR-DISK`) and `HDTV-480p` byte-identical (findings 4+9).
  - `tests/routes/goalsApplyPersistence.test.ts` (finding 14): run `_handleGoalApplyRequest` against a **real** in-memory `PCDCache` with a full seeded ladder; re-query `quality_profile_qualities` to prove the ladder + scoring persisted as previewed; **parity** — assert the ladder queries preview applied are byte-identical to the ladder portion of apply's merged op for the same server-derived `plan.ladderInput`; assert apply `configDiff === ` preview `configDiff` (finding 17). Also assert the merged op carries **both** `custom_format_scores` and `ordered_items` guard shapes (finding 7).
- **AC4/409** → `goalsRoutes.test.ts`:
  - **422:** a `quality_api_mappings` row whose `api_name` has no `QUALITIES` entry (ambiguous) and a straddling-group fixture → `assertRejects` + `getErrorStatus === 422`; re-query `quality_profile_qualities` unchanged (no partial write). Mirror the `lidarr → 400` / engine-version `409` templates (`:346-356`, `:450-463`).
  - **409:** simulate an upstream change between plan-read and persist so the guarded op matches 0 rows → `persistGoalApply` returns `success:false` → 409; re-query proves **neither** scoring **nor** ladder persisted (atomic).
- **AC5** → existing `engine.test.ts` golden CF-score maps and the "1080p ceiling demotes 2160p remux → -500" test (`:59-67`) still pass unchanged (only `engineVersion` updated + new input fields defaulted), proving score determinism and that a partial/empty ladder never blocks the CF plan.

---

## 8. Out of scope

- **Lidarr goal application** — `parseGoalRequest` continues to reject `lidarr` (400). `QUALITIES.lidarr` (audio, all resolution 0) is untouched.
- **Natural-language goal input** — none.
- **Automatic background reapplication of stale goal bindings** — bindings still upsert only on explicit apply.
- **Ladder reorder** — this design preserves existing `position` (enable/disable + cutoff only). Reorder is not performed.

---

## Resolved decisions (previously open questions)

1. **Reorder — NOT required.** #221's AC requires only an enabled set + cutoff; positions are preserved. If reorder is later requested, it is a separate change (a reorder would break the "zero removals / verbatim orphan" invariant's simplicity and needs its own cross-arr analysis).
2. **Scoring↔ladder atomicity — DECIDED: single combined op (option b).** Goal apply persists scoring + ladder as **one** `writeOperation` with merged `desiredState { custom_format_scores, ordered_items }` (§4.6), so the whole apply is atomic — no scoring-committed-ladder-failed partial. The value-guard gate is extended to evaluate both key families on one op (§5, finding 7). "No partial writes" now holds end-to-end.
3. **`GoalApplyResponse.configDiff` — DECIDED: add it** (finding 17). Apply echoes the persisted ladder+scoring diff from the same `buildQualityProfileConfigDiff` path; a test asserts it matches preview's `configDiff`.
4. **Group ceiling rule — DECIDED.** Decision uses **max resolution of the group's mapped, non-junk members**: enable iff `<= ceiling`; straddle among those members → 422 (`straddling_group`). Groups with **no** mapped non-junk member (all junk / all sibling-only) are **preserved verbatim, never fatal**. Verified against the real `create.ts` default profile (`WEB 1080p`, `Pre-releases`, `Unwanted`): no 422 on either arr (finding 1). The earlier "Bluray-1080p/Remux-1080p grouping" claim was factually wrong — those are individual qualities.
5. **Junk breadth — DECIDED.** `DVDSCR`/`REGIONAL` (and the other pre-release names) stay in `JUNK_QUALITIES` → disabled at any ceiling, and, when grouped (e.g. `Pre-releases`), the group is preserved verbatim rather than errored.
6. **`GoalLadderChange` granularity — DECIDED: two op-derived variants** (`set_quality_enabled`, `set_quality_cutoff`), each carrying `from`/`to`, defined in a **goals-only** schema (not the shared `ProposedChange` union — finding 11). `appliedChanges` is derived from emitted ops (1:1 with `configDiff`); a cutoff move is the pair of `upgradeUntil` transitions (finding 13). `configDiff` remains the authoritative field-level diff.
7. **Cutoff vs. preset intent — DECIDED.** Cutoff is fixed to `Bluray-<ceiling>` (issue frames cutoff as a function of ceiling only); presets do not push cutoff to `Remux-<ceiling>` in this slice.
8. **Cross-arr shared-ladder collisions — DECIDED: surface, don't silently guard** (finding 3). A per-arr ceiling reshapes the shared `quality_profile_qualities` row set for every syncing Arr; sequential sibling applies are legitimate last-write-wins and are **not** caught by the value guard. We surface `qualityLadder.reshapesSiblingArrs` + `sharedLadderNote` in preview and the decision log, and reserve the (write-time) value guard for genuine TOCTOU concurrent modifications (→ 409). We do **not** gate application on single-arr compatibility, because shared profiles are a legitimate configuration; the advisory keeps the reshape transparent.

---

## Appendix: critique changelog (blockers/majors folded in)

1. Blocker #1 (group rule hard-fails default profile): §3.5 rewritten so all-junk / all-unmapped-for-arr groups are preserved verbatim (non-fatal); removed the `unmapped_group_member` error code; added default-profile (create.ts: WEB 1080p / Pre-releases / Unwanted) golden apply tests for radarr+sonarr proving no 422; corrected the false 'Bluray-1080p/Remux-1080p grouping' claim.
2. Major #2 (inconsistent orphan handling): §3.4(A) makes standalone and grouped sibling-only qualities identical — both excluded from ceiling computation and preserved verbatim; fail-fast reserved for groups whose mapped non-junk members straddle the ceiling.
3. Major #3 (value guard doesn't catch sequential cross-arr collisions): dropped the false 'value guard prevents cross-arr collisions' claim; added §3.4(B) + qualityLadder.reshapesSiblingArrs/sharedLadderNote advisory surfaced in preview and decision log; clarified the guard is TOCTOU-only. Resolved decision 8.
4. Major #4 & #9 (omitting passthrough rows → DELETEs): added the full-coverage invariant (ladderInput.orderedItems === entire current ladder, orphans cloned verbatim) plus a defensive forbidRemovals option on the goal path; added a zero-removal cross-arr characterization test.
5. Minor #5 (materializer lowercasing/skip): §4.3 spells out exact-case fact.name from quality_name, resolution via QUALITIES[arrType][api_name], and throw-422 (not skip) on unresolved api_name; added a sonarr remux fact test (name==='Remux-1080p', resolution===1080).
6. Blocker #6 (guards fire at recompile, override overwrites): corrected the guard model in §5 (recompile-time + per-strategy); goal apply now persists via the runValueGuardGate:true path (verified in writer.ts:586-594 to reject pre-persist) and maps conflict to 409; removed cross-arr protection claims under the default override strategy.
7. Major #7 (two-write partial): §4.6/§5 merge scoring+ladder into ONE writeOperation with merged desiredState {custom_format_scores, ordered_items}; required the guard gate to evaluate both key families; resolved open question 2 to option (b).
8. Major #8 (preview can't surface conflicts / baseline divergence): §4.4 clones the sandbox from the resolved cache (matching apply's baseline) and adds a rowcount check → skippedChange on 0-row guarded UPDATEs.
9. Blocker #11 (widening shared union breaks impact input contract): §4.4 defines a goals-only GoalLadderChange union in goals.yaml referenced only by goal responses; impact-simulator.yaml left unchanged; describeChange made exhaustive; added a rejection contract test.
10. Major #12 (computeGoalPlan became partial, AC5 regression): §3.5/§4.2 make missing_cutoff and empty_enabled_set non-fatal (ladderInput=null, CF plan still returned); 422 reserved for ambiguous mapping + straddling group; added regression note on Movies fixture / planFor() staying 200.
11. Major #13 (appliedChanges diverge from configDiff): §4.4 derives appliedChanges from emitted ops (1:1 with configDiff) and represents the cutoff move as paired from/to set_quality_cutoff entries; variants gain from/to.
12. Major #14 (no persistence/parity test): §7 adds an integration apply test against a real in-memory PCDCache (re-queries to prove persistence) plus a preview↔apply ladder-op byte-parity test and apply-configDiff==preview assertion.
13. Minor #15 (dependency wiring / symbol name): §4.6/§6 add persistGoalApply to DEFAULT_DEPENDENCIES and the test buildApplyDependencies factory; corrected the handler symbol to _handleGoalApplyRequest.
14. Minor #16 (camelCase field): §7 AC2 asserts orderedItems["<name>"].upgradeUntil/enabled (camelCase); upgrade_until kept only for raw-SQL characterization.
15. Minor #17 (apply has no wire echo): added GoalApplyResponse.configDiff (resolved open question 3) with regen + a matches-preview assertion.
16. Open questions section converted to 'Resolved decisions' with concrete rulings for reorder, atomicity, configDiff, group rule, junk breadth, change granularity, cutoff intent, and cross-arr shared-ladder handling.
